text:
🇦🇺 AUSTRALIA — TAX STRUCTURE 
1. Why this matters
Defines tax exposure on rental income, property disposal, ownership holding costs, and transaction-related taxes for non-resident investors.
2. Scope Note
(Official Context)
This note applies to non-resident individual ownership of residential real estate and does not cover corporate/entity structures.
3. Federal Tax Position
Rental Income Tax
(Official Fact)
Rental income earned by non-residents is taxed at progressive income tax rates.
(Official Fact)
No tax-free threshold applies to non-residents
Tax applies from the first dollar of income
Top marginal rate: 45% (Medicare levy generally not applicable to non-residents)
Capital Gains Tax (CGT)
(Official Fact)
Capital gains from disposal of Australian real estate are taxed under the capital gains tax regime as part of income tax.
(Official Fact)
No CGT discount available for non-residents
Full capital gain is taxable at applicable non-resident income tax rates
(Clarification)
CGT is not a separate tax; it forms part of the income tax system.
Federal income tax (including capital gains) applies at the national level, while stamp duty, land tax, and foreign buyer surcharges are imposed at the state and territory level.
4. State and Territory Taxes / Charges
(Official Fact)
Property-related taxes and charges are imposed at the state and territory level, and vary by jurisdiction.
Stamp Duty (Transfer Duty)
Payable on acquisition of property
Rates vary by state
Foreign Buyer Surcharge Duty
Additional duty applied to foreign purchasers
Example: approximately 8% in New South Wales (varies by state)
Land Tax
Annual tax on property ownership
Higher rates or surcharges often apply to foreign owners
5. Property-Related Ongoing Costs
(Official Fact)
Land tax (state-level)
Council rates (local government charges)
(Advisory Note)
These are ownership-related costs, not income taxes.
6. Taxes Not Imposed
(Official Fact)
Australia does not impose estate or inheritance tax on property ownership.
7. Advisory Caution
(Advisory Caution)
Tax treatment differs between:
Federal (income and CGT)
State (stamp duty, land tax, surcharges)
Foreign investors are typically subject to:
Higher state-level charges
No CGT discount
8. What Needs Legal Confirmation (Live Case)
Applicable state-level stamp duty and surcharge rates
Land tax obligations for specific property
Tax residency classification
Deduction eligibility and taxable income calculation
9. Sources
https://www.ato.gov.au
https://foreigninvestment.gov.au
10. Verification Date
09/04/2026
11. Escalation Note
Not Required –
Tax framework is clearly defined at both federal and state levels. Variations arise from jurisdiction and transaction specifics, not from unresolved legal ambiguity.
